commands.description: Ensure raw input is never concatenated into SQL strings. Use parameterized queries/prepared statements.
commands.mistake: Using template literals: `SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${req.query.id}`, which exposes database schemas directly to injection payloads.

commands.description: Escape all untrusted data before rendering it in the HTML DOM, or use libraries that do it automatically.
commands.mistake: Using dangerouslySetInnerHTML or innerHTML directly on raw inputs from search query parameters or user profiles.

commands.description: Restricts resources (such as JavaScript, CSS, Images) that the browser is allowed to load for a given page.
commands.mistake: Using 'unsafe-inline' or '*' in CSP directives, which completely defeats the anti-XSS features of CSP.

commands.description: Forces browser connections to happen securely over HTTPS instead of HTTP.
commands.mistake: Enabling HSTS with a short max-age duration or without testing subdomains, potentially locking out users from legacy HTTP APIs.

commands.description: Never store plain text passwords. Use standard slow hashing functions like bcrypt or Argon2 with high cost factors.
commands.example:
const hash = await bcrypt.hash(rawPassword, 12);
const matches = await bcrypt.compare(inputPassword, hash);
commands.mistake: Using fast hashing algorithms like MD5, SHA-1, or SHA-256 for passwords, which are easily cracked via dictionary attacks or GPUs.

commands.description: Use Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) with Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) for authenticated encryption/decryption of private text.
commands.mistake: Reusing Initialization Vectors (IVs) or using insecure encryption modes like AES-ECB, which leak patterns in encrypted data.
